factors.summary: Demand trends are decelerating, particularly in energy and industrial sectors.

evidence.text: We experienced declines in project orders, particularly within the energy and industrial businesses. We expect headwinds in the energy and industrial business to continue and oil prices to remain depressed throughout 2016.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are decelerating in key sectors, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: The company is selectively increasing prices to mitigate inflationary costs, but pricing pressures exist.

evidence.text: •selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is taking action to mitigate costs through selective price increases.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is present, though tempered by external factors.

evidence.text: The 4.3 and 5.5 percent increase s in consolidated net sales in the second quarter and first half , respectively, of 2016 from 2015 were primarily driven by: •core sales growth in Water Quality Systems and Technical Solutions, primarily as the result of increased volume in the United States and Canada.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: There is organic growth, but it is tempered by external factors, leading to mixed signals.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with growth in some segments but declines in others.

evidence.text: The 4.3 and 5.5 percent increase s in consolidated net sales in the second quarter and first half , respectively, of 2016 from 2015 were primarily driven by: •core sales growth in Water Quality Systems and Technical Solutions, primarily as the result of increased volume in the United States and Canada. These increase s were partially offset by: •continued slowdown in industrial capital spending, particularly in the oil & gas and energy-related businesses, driving core sales declines in Valves & Controls; •slowing economic activity in certain developing regions, including China and Brazil; and •a strong U.S. dollar causing unfavorable foreign currency effects.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends are mixed, with growth in some segments offset by declines in others.

factors.summary: End markets are mixed, with growth in water filtration and technical solutions but declines in energy and industrial sectors.

evidence.text: •core sales growth in Water Quality Systems and Technical Solutions, primarily as the result of increased volume in the United States and Canada. These increase s were partially offset by: •continued slowdown in industrial capital spending, particularly in the oil & gas and energy-related businesses, driving core sales declines in Valves & Controls; •slowing economic activity in certain developing regions, including China and Brazil; and •a strong U.S. dollar causing unfavorable foreign currency effects.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End markets show mixed performance, with growth in some areas and declines in others.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, with the company attempting to offset through pricing and productivity.

evidence.text: •inflationary increases related to raw materials and labor costs. This increase was partially offset by: •higher contribution margin as a result of savings generated from our Pentair Integrated Management System ('PIMS') initiatives including lean and supply management practices; and •selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising, contributing to margin pressure despite offsetting measures.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure.

evidence.text: •inflationary increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, leading to margin pressure.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure due to various factors, including inflation and foreign exchange effects.

evidence.text: The 0.5 percentage point increase in gross profit as a percentage of sales in the second quarter of 2016 from 2015 was primarily driven by: •higher contribution margin as a result of savings generated from our Pentair Integrated Management System ('PIMS') initiatives including lean and supply management practices; and •selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases. This increase was partially offset by: •inflationary increases related to raw materials and labor costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under pressure due to inflation and foreign exchange effects.

factors.summary: Restructuring actions are ongoing and expected to contribute to margin growth.

evidence.text: We initiated restructuring actions to offset the negative earnings impact of foreign exchange and core revenue decline. We expect to continue these actions throughout 2016 and that these actions will contribute to margin growth in 2016.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring actions are expected to contribute to margin growth, indicating a positive development.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through lean and supply management initiatives.

evidence.text: •higher contribution margin as a result of savings generated from our Pentair Integrated Management System ('PIMS') initiatives including lean and supply management practices; and •selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity through lean and supply management initiatives shows significant positive development.

factors.summary: Management is investing in growth initiatives, indicating confidence.

evidence.text: •increased investment in sales and marketing to drive growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in growth initiatives reflects management's confidence in future prospects.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic slowdowns, foreign exchange effects, and oil price volatility.

evidence.text: •We expect this trend to continue throughout 2016. •We experienced declines in project orders, particularly within the energy and industrial businesses. We expect headwinds in the energy and industrial business to continue and oil prices to remain depressed throughout 2016.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks like economic slowdowns and oil price volatility pose significant challenges.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic goals are clear, focusing on innovation and market expansion.

evidence.text: •Optimizing our technological capabilities to increasingly generate innovative new products; and •Focusing on developing global talent in light of our increased global presence.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategic goals focused on innovation and market expansion indicate positive direction.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange rates, oil prices, and economic conditions.

evidence.text: These factors include overall global economic and business conditions, including worldwide demand for oil and gas; the ability to achieve the benefits of our restructuring plans; the ability to successfully identify, finance, complete and integrate acquisitions, including the ability to successfully integrate and achieve the expected benefits of the acquisition of ERICO Global Company; competition and pricing pressures in the markets we serve; the strength of housing and related markets; volatility in currency exchange rates and commodity prices; inability to generate savings from excellence in operations initiatives consisting of lean enterprise, supply management and cash flow practices; increased risks associated with operating foreign businesses; the ability to deliver backlog and win future project work; failure of markets to accept new product introductions and enhancements; the impact of changes in laws and regulations, including those that limit U.S. tax benefits; the outcome of litigation and governmental proceedings; and the ability to achieve our long-term strategic operating goals.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Exposure to foreign exchange rates, oil prices, and economic conditions presents significant risks.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product introductions and integration synergies from acquisitions.

evidence.text: •We have identified specific product and geographic market opportunities that we find attractive and continue to pursue, both within and outside the United States. We are reinforcing our businesses to more effectively address these opportunities through research and development and additional sales and marketing resources. Unless we successfully penetrate these markets, our core sales growth will likely be limited or may decline.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product introductions and integration synergies from acquisitions are positive catalysts for growth.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, reflecting investment in growth.

evidence.text: •Driving operating excellence through lean enterprise initiatives, with specific focus on sourcing and supply management, cash flow management and lean operations; •Achieving differentiated revenue growth through new products and global and market expansion; •Optimizing our technological capabilities to increasingly generate innovative new products; and •Focusing on developing global talent in light of our increased global presence.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures reflect investment in growth opportunities, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing, driven by higher debt levels and interest rates.

evidence.text: The 90.9 and 95.4 percent increase s in net interest expense in the second quarter and first half of 2016 from 2015 were primarily driven by: •the impact of higher debt levels during the first six months of 2016, compared to the first six months of 2015, primarily as the result of the September 2015 issuance of senior notes to finance the ERICO Acquisition; and •increased overall interest rates in effect on our outstanding debt.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher debt levels and increased interest rates are leading to significantly higher net interest expenses, indicating financial strain.

factors.summary: MA activity is positive, with successful integration of ERICO.

evidence.text: •net sales of $141.9 million and $262.0 million for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016 , respectively, as a result of the ERICO Acquisition; and •core sales growth in Water Quality Systems and Technical Solutions, primarily as the result of increased volume in the United States and Canada.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The successful integration of ERICO and core sales growth in other areas demonstrate positive momentum.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind, with global slowdowns and foreign exchange effects impacting performance.

evidence.text: •Our results were negatively impacted due to the strengthening of the U.S. dollar against most key global currencies. We expect this trend to continue throughout 2016. •We experienced declines in project orders, particularly within the energy and industrial businesses. We expect headwinds in the energy and industrial business to continue and oil prices to remain depressed throughout 2016.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: A global slowdown and unfavorable foreign exchange effects are negatively impacting performance.

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are increasing debt service costs.

evidence.text: The 90.9 and 95.4 percent increase s in net interest expense in the second quarter and first half of 2016 from 2015 were primarily driven by: •the impact of higher debt levels during the first six months of 2016, compared to the first six months of 2015, primarily as the result of the September 2015 issuance of senior notes to finance the ERICO Acquisition; and •increased overall interest rates in effect on our outstanding debt.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher interest rates are increasing debt service costs, leading to significant financial strain.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange exposure is negative, with the strong U.S. dollar causing unfavorable effects.

evidence.text: •a strong U.S. dollar causing unfavorable foreign currency effects.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The strong U.S. dollar is causing unfavorable foreign currency effects, negatively impacting performance.
